text.title: devops-platform-crossplane Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for devops-platform-crossplane. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every Crossplane XRD MUST define both a composite type (XDatabase) and a claim type (Database). The claim is what developers interact with in their namespace. Compositions MUST embed security defaults that cannot be overridden by claim parameters — storageEncrypted: true, publiclyAccessible: false, and deleteProtection: false in non-production are non-negotiable platform defaults. Developers MUST NOT be able to set storageEncrypted: false through the claim API.
rule.rationale: Security defaults embedded in the Composition layer are enforced at the platform level, not through policy review. This achieves near-100% security compliance (Netflix Wall-E model) by making insecure configurations impossible to request, not forbidden by policy.

rule.statement: Platform Terraform modules MUST set cluster_endpoint_public_access = false and cluster_endpoint_private_access = true by default for all Kubernetes cluster modules. Public endpoint access MUST NOT be the default. Modules MUST output cluster_endpoint and cluster_certificate_authority_data for downstream consumption without requiring module consumers to access the provider state directly.
rule.rationale: Public Kubernetes API endpoints are the most common misconfiguration leading to cluster compromise. Embedding the private-only default in the module means all EKS clusters provisioned through the platform golden path are secure by construction, not by policy reminder.
